title: Reverse Proxies, Forwarded Trust, Message Framing, and Smuggling Defense · Stakeholder Go Mission
summary: A payment and fulfillment integration team. It needs exact CORS and CSRF behavior, secure cookies, signed replay-resistant webhooks, bounded egress, abuse protection, trusted forwarding, Rewrite-based proxying, and parser-consistency defense. Planned artifact: Deliver Browser-Safe Webhook and Reverse Proxy Gateway with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debuggin…
objectives: Use safe-method invariants, Go CrossOriginProtection, Sec-Fetch-Site and Origin policy, tokens when needed, and narrowly reviewed bypasses.
Bound and retain exact raw body bytes, select expected signature scheme and key, compare in constant time, and only then parse and authorize the event.
Treat server-side URLs, redirects, DNS results, IP ranges, schemes, ports, credentials, and response size as egress policy enforced on every hop.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go server Commit to the mission model · Project Csrf Crossoriginprotection
steps.instruction:
project case — A project team handles case 1744 in a canary service revision draining long-lived streams. Build deterministic Go evidence to reject ambiguous framing and deprecated Director-style proxy mutation; then make specification and runtime disagree on one nullable field and block release. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Use safe-method invariants, Go CrossOriginProtection, Sec-Fetch-Site and Origin policy, tokens when needed, and narrowly reviewed bypasses.

options.text: For test an acceptance boundary, run one convenient Framing Smuggling Consistency happy path, omit method, authority, credential, byte, time, redirect, retry, and cleanup boundaries, then accept plausible output.
Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Framing Smuggling Consistency condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.
Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder. That claim does not survive the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary.

steps.title: Go server Defend the stakeholder delivery · Project Framing Smuggling Consistency
steps.instruction:
Write the project evidence note for Framing Smuggling Consistency: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A project team handles case 1753 in a signed fulfillment webhook gateway under replay attack. Build deterministic Go evidence to reject ambiguous framing and deprecated Director-style proxy mutation; then repeat the request with a different method and retain status plus durable-effect evidence. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Prevent request smuggling by rejecting ambiguous Content-Length and Transfer-Encoding, malformed whitespace, conflicting parsers, oversized fields, and unsafe downgrade paths per RFC 9112.

Evidence target: Go server Defend the stakeholder delivery · Project Framing Smuggling Consistency.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Go server Defend the stakeholder delivery · Project Framing Smuggling Consistency, keep Framing Smuggling Consistency connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Prevent request smuggling by rejecting ambiguous Content-Length and Transfer-Encoding, malformed whitespace, conflicting parsers, oversized fields, and unsafe downgrade paths per RFC 9112.

hints: For Go server Defend the stakeholder delivery · Project Framing Smuggling Consistency, name the method, authority, body, byte, time, and credential boundary, Framing Smuggling Consistency precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Go server Defend the stakeholder delivery · Project Framing Smuggling Consistency, trace this concrete input and predict resolution, redirect, status, representation, retry, and cleanup evidence before reviewing it: A project team handles case 1753 in a signed fulfillment webhook gateway under replay attack. Build deterministic Go evidence to reject ambiguous framing and deprecated Director-style proxy mutation; then repeat the request with a different method and retain status plus durable-effect evidence. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Prevent request smuggling by rejecting ambiguous Content-Length and Transfer-Encoding, malformed whitespace, conflicting parsers, oversized fields, and unsafe downgrade paths per RFC 9112.

For Go server Defend the stakeholder delivery · Project Framing Smuggling Consistency, challenge the documented misconception with request, response, fault, policy, and transfer evidence: Because Go parses the front-end request safely, every downstream proxy and application will interpret it identically.
